articles.title: Hezbollah says it carried out 22 attacks on Israeli forces in south Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah says it carried out 22 attacks on Israeli forces in south Lebanon Hezbollah said it carried out 22 attacks against Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Saturday, targeting soldiers, tanks and military positions with attack drones, rocket barrages and artillery fire. According to the group's end-of-day statement, attacks were launched against Israeli forces in Odaisseh and Wadi Hunin near the border, as well as in al-Qantara, Bint Jbeil, Rashaf and around Beaufort Castle in Nabatieh, which Israeli forces seized earlier this week. Hezbollah also claimed to have targeted Israeli soldie

articles.title: Pope Leo says war on Iran fails Catholic ‘just war’ criteria
articles.summary: Pope Leo says war on Iran fails Catholic ‘just war’ criteria Pope Leo said the US-Israel war on Iran does not meet the standards of a “just war” under Catholic teaching, reiterating a position he has previously expressed during a visit to Madrid. Responding to reporters, the head of the Catholic Church said: “In Iran, the criteria for a just war are not present.” He added that traditional just war theory was developed in an era when humanity could not imagine the destructive power of modern weapons. articles.title: Israeli soldier kills seven-month-old Palestinian baby, then walks away
articles.summary: Israeli soldier kills seven-month-old Palestinian baby, then walks away Israeli  soldiers killed a seven-month-old  Palestinian  baby in the occupied West Bank after opening fire on his family’s car, despite the vehicle stopping when ordered. The baby boy, named Sam Fahd Abu Haikal, was shot on Friday afternoon in the Tel Rumeida area of Hebron while travelling with his parents, his 11-year-old brother and his grandmother. He was taken to hospital in critical condition and later died. Friday was the day he turned seven months old. His grandmother, Ferial Abu Haikal, told Re

articles.title: Palestinian detained after defending himself from Israeli settler attack
articles.summary: Palestinian detained after defending himself from Israeli settler attack Israeli forces detained a Palestinian driver after he defended himself during an attack by Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank on Saturday. The incident came after settlers threw rocks at vehicles in the south of Bethlehem, following an attempt by Israeli forces to evacuate an illegal settler outpost, Haaretz reported. The army said an Israeli settler was lightly wounded in the head when the Palestinian driver’s vehicle hit him during the attack. articles.title: France pushes sanctions over West Bank settler violence
articles.summary: France pushes sanctions over West Bank settler violence France is working with several countries on coordinated sanctions targeting people linked to violence in the occupied West Bank, three European diplomats said. The measures under discussion include asset freezes and travel bans, with announcements possible within days. Countries may still adopt different sanctions lists, as European governments move outside the EU framework after failing to secure unanimous support for tougher action against Israel. articles.title: Iran World Cup squad faces same-day US entry rule
articles.summary: Iran World Cup squad faces same-day US entry rule Iran’s World Cup squad must enter and leave the United States on the same day for matches played on US soil, Iran’s ambassador to Mexico says. The restrictions come as the Israeli-US war on Tehran spills into sport, forcing Iran to base its team in Mexico rather than its planned camp in Tucson, Arizona. “We can enter in the morning and we must leave the same day,” Iran’s envoy Abolfazl Pasandideh told reporters, describing the visa limits imposed on the squad. articles.title: Israeli army says two soldiers killed in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israeli army says two soldiers killed in southern Lebanon The Israeli army says two of its soldiers have been killed in southern Lebanon, where its forces continue operations despite the heavy toll Israel’s attacks have inflicted on Lebanese towns and villages. The military said one soldier died after suffering serious injuries in the area. It said another soldier was killed during what it described as “an operational activity” in southern Lebanon. The latest deaths take the number of Israeli soldiers killed in the latest war in Lebanon to 30.

The trip comes as Pakistan works to mediate talks aimed at stopping the war, with Israel’s ongoing attacks on Lebanon among the key points of tension.

articles.summary: Death toll from Israeli attack on Gaza City rises Wafa news agency is reporting that at least six people have been killed in an Israeli attack on a tent in Gaza City. Al Jazeera reported earlier that at least five were killed and 15 others were wounded when two Israeli missiles struck a tent where people had gathered to celebrate a wedding. Wafa said that the strike targeted a tent sheltering displaced families near al-Rimal boys' school, west of Gaza City. It added that most of the wounded were children.

articles.title: Israeli attack kills at least five in Gaza City
articles.summary: Israeli attack kills at least five in Gaza City At least five people, including women and children, were killed in an Israeli attack on a wedding tent in Gaza City, Al Jazeera is reporting. According to the report, two missiles struck the tent, which was "packed" with people who had gathered for a wedding. It said that shrapnel "flew at a very high speed" into a neighbouring displacement site school where families were sheltering. It said that the number of fatalities is expected to rise, citing a source at al-Shifa Hospital reporting that around 15 others were wounded in the attack.

articles.summary: Lebanese president: Israel 'will never be able to achieve their objective' Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has said in an interview with CNN that Israel "will never be able to achieve their objective" of defeating Hezbollah. articles.title: How Bolivia's Plans to Tap Mineral Riches Are Being Tested by Mass Unrest
articles.summary: Bolivia’s new president, Rodrigo Paz, is attempting to rebrand the country as a destination for foreign investment after two decades of socialist rule. Sitting atop some of the world’s largest lithium resources and vast mineral reserves, Bolivia has long struggled to translate its natural resources into economic prosperity. Paz argues that clearer laws, stability, and closer ties with international investors can unlock growth. But, just six months into President Paz’s term, his hopes are meeting the reality of unrest at home and violent protests. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Broadway Shows Eye London, NY Wooing With Tax Credits
articles.summary: Catching a Broadway musical is a quintessential experience for many in New York City. But it’s not cheap, not for showgoers nor for show producers, who saw costs go up in the aftermath of the pandemic. Producers have been considering taking their next big hits somewhere else — to Atlanta, Chicago and even overseas to London, where it can be significantly cheaper to put on a good show. Bloomberg Tax, State Tax Policy Journalist Danielle Muoio Dunn joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
